my_thoughts: 'But it hurt'—they said it. Three turns from defending ('didn't mean it,' 'was stressed') to owning impact ('it hurt'). Teacher validates simply: 'Yes, it hurt.' No analysis, just validation. This confirms: your hurt is what matters, not his reasons. Three-turn progression from excuse to acknowledgment. Breakthrough.
accumulated_insight: T3: Student admits 'But it hurt.' From defending abuser (T1-2) to acknowledging impact (T3). Teacher validates: 'Yes, it hurt. That's what matters.' Reinforces impact over excuse. Predicting surface_emotion as admission deepens.
visible_response: And how many times did you remind yourself of it and call yourself stupid?
